Green Party — Economy / "A tax system for all of us" (current, from greens.org.nz/tax_system_for_all)

The Vision: A warm, safe home, affordable food and power, well-resourced schools, quality health care when you need it, and a thriving environment that underpins it all — the building blocks of a healthy society, fair and within reach.

Why it matters: Our economy is the largest it's ever been. There is more than enough in Aotearoa for everyone to live a good life, but right now it doesn't feel that way — critical social services are failing, the cost of the basics is through the roof, and so are the profits of the wealthiest.

Green approach: redesign the tax system so it is fair for everyone — those at the top and big profits pay their fair share, and that revenue funds the essentials (housing, health, education, income support, climate action). The Green economy exists to support the wellbeing of people and the planet, not just growth for its own right. (See also Green Fiscal Strategy and Green Budget 2025 alternative budget.) A guaranteed minimum income / income support so nobody lives in poverty.
